evals.prompt: I have a Java service that makes 3 API calls and I want them to run in parallel. What's the modern Java 21 way to do this?
evals.expected_output: Use StructuredTaskScope.ShutdownOnFailure(). Fork each call, scope.join().throwIfFailed(), then get results. Treats all tasks as a single logical unit with automatic cancellation on failure.

evals.prompt: My Java REST server handles thousands of concurrent requests. Should I use a thread pool or Virtual Threads?
evals.expected_output: Use Virtual Threads — Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor(). They're lightweight (cheap to create, cheap to block), ideal for I/O-bound workloads. Do NOT pool them — just create one per task.

evals.prompt: I'm using synchronized blocks with blocking I/O inside a Java Virtual Thread app. My colleague says this is a problem. Why?
evals.expected_output: Blocking inside synchronized pins the carrier thread in Loom — defeats the purpose of virtual threads. Use ReentrantLock instead of synchronized when blocking I/O is needed inside a lock.
